ideas.title: I Carried A 100-Lb Sandbag For 10 Miles — The Mile Counter Froze At 10.3 miles
ideas.one_line_premise: I carry a 100-lb sandbag on my shoulder on a long stretch of road, narrating every sensation in my foot until the mile counter freezes at 10.3 miles.

concept.title: I Carried A 100-Lb Sandbag For 10 Miles — The Mile Counter Froze At 10.3 miles
concept.logline: I carry a 100-lb sandbag on my shoulder on a long stretch of road, narrating every sensation in my foot until the mile counter freezes at 10.3 miles.
concept.promise: You're watching me carry a 100-lb sandbag on my shoulder — the question is where the counter lands on a specific number.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the mile counter freezes at 10.3 miles; a single sensation word appears as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook implies a round target (10 miles); payoff lands on a specific, non-round value (10.3 miles) — specificity over-delivers vs the rounded promise.

opening.first_frame: Close on sandbag heaved onto my shoulder mid-step. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — 10 miles, and my foot is going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on the bag carried step by step with a distance overlay for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter a long stretch of road; the bag carried step by step with a distance overlay; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first foot sensation narration ("my foot is bigger"); counter begins to tick
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating the bag carried step by step with a distance overlay with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the mile counter freezes at 10.3 miles; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the mile counter freezes at 10.3 miles; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with foot + impact word: "foot's numb — insane."

mid: alternate foot close-ups with the bag carried step by step with a distance overlay

Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

ideas.title: I Trained With A Pro Boxer For One Full Day — He Told Me When To Stop
ideas.one_line_premise: I shadow a professional boxer through every single thing he does in his training day — warm-up, bag work, sparring, ice, food inside a pro fighter’s actual gym, narrating every sensation in my shoulders until the person I shadowed decides out loud when my day is over.

concept.title: I Trained With A Pro Boxer For One Full Day — He Told Me When To Stop
concept.logline: I shadow a professional boxer through every single thing he does in his training day — warm-up, bag work, sparring, ice, food inside a pro fighter’s actual gym, narrating every sensation in my shoulders until the person I shadowed decides out loud when my day is over.
concept.promise: You're watching me shadow a professional boxer through every single thing he does in his training day — warm-up, bag work, sparring, ice, food — the question is where the person I shadowed says we’re done.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the person I shadowed decides out loud when my day is over; a single word of reaction lands as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook implies a legible answer to the premise (2 days); payoff delivers the reveal as one frame/phrase — specificity of the single shot over-delivers vs the rounded premise.

opening.first_frame: Close on a pro boxer wrapping my hands with his gym behind him in the same frame. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — 2 days, and my shoulders are going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on matching his bag work beat for beat with both fighters in the shot for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter inside a pro fighter’s actual gym; matching his bag work beat for beat with both fighters in the shot; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first shoulders sensation narration ("my shoulders is bigger"); counter begins to tick
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating matching his bag work beat for beat with both fighters in the shot with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the person I shadowed decides out loud when my day is over; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the person I shadowed decides out loud when my day is over; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with shoulders + impact word: "shoulders's numb — insane."

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

ideas.title: I Ran The Same Mile Every Day For 365 Days — The First And Last Run Are The Same Shot
ideas.one_line_premise: I run the same neighborhood mile every morning and film the same starting frame on day 1 and day N on the exact same one-mile neighborhood loop, narrating every sensation in my foot until the timer hits exactly 365 days.

concept.title: I Ran The Same Mile Every Day For 365 Days — The First And Last Run Are The Same Shot
concept.logline: I run the same neighborhood mile every morning and film the same starting frame on day 1 and day N on the exact same one-mile neighborhood loop, narrating every sensation in my foot until the timer hits exactly 365 days.
concept.promise: You're watching me run the same neighborhood mile every morning and film the same starting frame on day 1 and day N — the question is where the counter lands on a specific number.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the timer hits exactly 365 days; a single sensation word appears as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook implies a round target (365 days); payoff lands on a specific, non-round value (365 days) — specificity over-delivers vs the rounded promise.

opening.first_frame: Close on the exact same driveway starting frame with a big day-counter label in the corner. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — 365 days, and my foot is going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on a cut between two runs at the same starting line on day 1 and day N for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter the exact same one-mile neighborhood loop; a cut between two runs at the same starting line on day 1 and day N; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first foot sensation narration ("my foot is bigger"); counter begins to tick
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating a cut between two runs at the same starting line on day 1 and day N with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the timer hits exactly 365 days; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the timer hits exactly 365 days; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with foot + impact word: "foot's numb — insane."

first_5s: the exact same driveway starting frame with a big day-counter label in the corner
hook_quarter: a cut between two runs at the same starting line on day 1 and day N
mid: alternate foot close-ups with a cut between two runs at the same starting line on day 1 and day N
text overlay at beat moments only

ideas.title: I Tried To Learn 500 Words Of A New Language In One Day — A Native Speaker Tested Me At The End
ideas.one_line_premise: I drill flashcards in a language I don’t speak from morning until night, then sit with a native speaker who quizzes every word at a kitchen table with a native speaker sitting opposite me at dusk, narrating every sensation in my feeling until the counter freezes at 514.

concept.title: I Tried To Learn 500 Words Of A New Language In One Day — A Native Speaker Tested Me At The End
concept.logline: I drill flashcards in a language I don’t speak from morning until night, then sit with a native speaker who quizzes every word at a kitchen table with a native speaker sitting opposite me at dusk, narrating every sensation in my feeling until the counter freezes at 514.
concept.promise: You're watching me drill flashcards in a language I don’t speak from morning until night, then sit with a native speaker who quizzes every word — the question is where the counter lands on a specific number.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the counter freezes at 514; a single sensation word appears as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook implies a round target (500); payoff lands on a specific, non-round value (514) — specificity over-delivers vs the rounded promise.

opening.first_frame: Close on a stack of flashcards with a single unfamiliar word on the top card and a count overlay at zero. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — 500, and my feeling is going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on flashcards moving from an unknown-pile to a known-pile with a running tally for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter a kitchen table with a native speaker sitting opposite me at dusk; flashcards moving from an unknown-pile to a known-pile with a running tally; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first feeling sensation narration ("my feeling is bigger"); counter begins to tick
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating flashcards moving from an unknown-pile to a known-pile with a running tally with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the counter freezes at 514; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the counter freezes at 514; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with feeling + impact word: "feeling's numb — insane."

ideas.title: I Built A Boat Out Of Only Cardboard And Tape And Rowed It 2 Miles — The Build Held Until 2.3 miles
ideas.one_line_premise: I build a boat out of only cardboard and packing tape, then row it across a lake as far as the seams hold on a calm shallow lake wearing a life vest, narrating every sensation in my shoulders until the build holds until 2.3 miles and then visibly gives.

concept.title: I Built A Boat Out Of Only Cardboard And Tape And Rowed It 2 Miles — The Build Held Until 2.3 miles
concept.logline: I build a boat out of only cardboard and packing tape, then row it across a lake as far as the seams hold on a calm shallow lake wearing a life vest, narrating every sensation in my shoulders until the build holds until 2.3 miles and then visibly gives.
concept.promise: You're watching me build a boat out of only cardboard and packing tape, then row it across a lake as far as the seams hold — the question is where the build holds or visibly gives.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the build holds until 2.3 miles and then visibly gives; a single sensation word appears as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook implies a round target (2 miles); payoff lands on a specific, non-round value (2.3 miles) — specificity over-delivers vs the rounded promise.

opening.first_frame: Close on taping the last cardboard seam on a finished hull with the shoreline framing the shot. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — 2 miles, and my shoulders are going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on oars pulling as the cardboard boat slides out from the dock for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter a calm shallow lake wearing a life vest; oars pulling as the cardboard boat slides out from the dock; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first shoulders sensation narration ("my shoulders is bigger"); counter begins to tick
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating oars pulling as the cardboard boat slides out from the dock with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the build holds until 2.3 miles and then visibly gives; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the build holds until 2.3 miles and then visibly gives; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with shoulders + impact word: "shoulders's numb — insane."

build_phases.rationale: Build phases are validated against the top-5 retention predictors + narrative_arc_analysis (best/worst arc labels) + wave7 quartile templates.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

why_it_works: Specific premise (I Built A Boat Out Of Only Cardboard And Tape And Rowed It 2 Miles — The Build Held Unt…) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 9 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.
Validation trace: 16 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).
